commutator_effect: The radial commutator is nonhomogeneous in v and can inject positive forcing at the crossing unless a pointwise sign or a quantitative dominance margin is supplied.
domination_hypothesis: Either -(partial_z u^r) partial_r u1 <= 0 at every admissible first crossing, or its positive part is absorbed by a log-aware barrier margin.
local_condition: At a first interior positive maximum of v=partial_z u1, v>0, partial_r v=partial_z v=0, and Ltilde v<=0.
required_evidence: D_t v = nu Ltilde v + 2 v partial_z psi1 + 2 u1 partial_z^2 psi1 - (partial_z u^r) partial_r u1 - (partial_z u^z) v
first-crossing maximum-principle geometry
admissible crossing set away from unhandled boundary defects

commutator_effect: Under this conditional sign, the radial commutator cannot create the first positive v crossing.
domination_hypothesis: pointwise favorable sign at all possible crossings

no Biot-Savart sign preservation theorem recorded

persistence through the Hou-Luo scaling window

required_evidence: correlation estimate for partial_z u^r and partial_r u1

commutator_effect: A strict pointwise margin would absorb positive commutator bursts before v crosses above zero.
